title: Criminal Law Expert Witness | Psychologists & Psychiatrists
description: Fitness to plead, risk assessments, and mental health reports for criminal courts. Fast CVs, Legal Aid rates guaranteed.

blocks.headline: Meet Your Criminal Expert Witness Fast
blocks.subtitle: Whether you need a fitness to plead assessment, risk evaluation, or pre-sentencing report, we'll match you with an expert in 24 hours. All our psychologists and psychiatrists work at Legal Aid rates.
blocks.secondaryCtaText: What Does a Fitness to Plead Assessment Cover?

tabs.label: Psychology Services
tabs.content:
Our forensic psychologists deliver expert psychological assessments that stand up in court. Every psychologist on our register is HCPC-registered, trained in forensic assessment, and experienced in defending their findings under cross-examination.

Psychology services for criminal cases include:

- Fitness to plead assessments (Pritchard criteria)
- Mental capacity and criminal responsibility evaluations
- Risk of re-offending assessments
- Suggestibility and false confession evaluations
- Witness reliability assessments
- IQ and cognitive functioning assessments
- Neuropsychological evaluations
- Mental health and personality assessments
- Sexual offender risk assessments
- Pre-sentencing psychological reports
- Prison parole and IPP assessments

tabs.label: Psychiatry Services
tabs.content:
Our GMC-registered, Section 12 approved psychiatrists deliver expert opinion that shapes sentencing and parole outcomes. They assess mental state at the time of offence, diagnose mental health conditions, and recommend treatment â€” all backed by clinical expertise.

Psychiatry services for criminal cases include:

- Fitness to plead and fitness to stand trial (Pritchard criteria)
- Mental capacity assessments
- Psychiatric assessment of mental state at time of offence
- Criminal responsibility and mens rea evaluations
- Risk of re-offending assessments
- Sexual and violent offender evaluations
- Personality disorder and psychosis assessments
- PTSD and trauma-related evaluations
- Substance misuse and dependency assessments
- Neuropsychiatric assessments
- Pre-sentencing and probation reports
- Parole hearing and IPP assessments
- Treatment recommendations and prognosis

tabs.label: Working Together
tabs.content:
Fitness to plead assessments often require both psychologist and psychiatrist input. A psychologist assesses cognitive functioning and suggestibility; a psychiatrist assesses mental state and medical factors. For risk assessments, a psychologist evaluates behaviour and psychology; a psychiatrist assesses mental health and medical aspects. Your Client Manager will advise on the best combination for your case.

Not sure? Use our tool: Psychologist or Psychiatrist? â†â€™ /tools/psychologist-or-psychiatrist/

blocks.heading: Expertise You Can Trust
blocks.bodyText: Our forensic psychologists and psychiatrists have decades of combined experience in criminal courts. They understand cross-examination, Pritchard criteria, and what judges need to see. Every expert is vetted for forensic credentials and courtroom experience before joining our network.

blocks.heading: Expert Assessments That Win Cases

cards.title: Fitness to Plead Assessments
cards.description: Pritchard criteria assessments for defendants lacking capacity to participate in proceedings. Urgent turnaround available.

cards.title: Witness Reliability & Suggestibility

steps.description: Pick your expert and we take it from there. We manage appointments, correspond with other parties, and handle all admin. Your case stays on track.

steps.title: Court-Ready Report Delivered
steps.description: Your report gets 100% quality-checked and proofread. We verify it meets your letter of instruction and court standards. Standard turnaround: 4â€“6 weeks; fast-track available.

faqs.question: What is a Fitness to Plead assessment?
faqs.answer: Fitness to plead is a court assessment of whether a defendant has the mental capacity to participate in their trial. The Pritchard criteria evaluates four key capacities: understanding the charge, understanding guilty vs. not guilty pleas, instructing a solicitor, and following court proceedings. If a defendant fails these, the court may stay proceedings or order psychiatric treatment.

faqs.question: Who can assess Fitness to Plead?
faqs.answer: Under the Mental Health Act, fitness to plead assessments must be carried out by a psychiatrist or psychologist with appropriate training. Section 12 approval is not required for fitness to plead (though many of our psychiatrists are Section 12 approved). We have extensive experience in this area.

faqs.question: How quickly can you provide an expert?
faqs.answer: For criminal cases, we prioritize urgent requests. We typically provide 2â€“3 CVs within 24 hours. For truly urgent fitness to plead assessments, we can sometimes offer same-day expert matching. Call 01306 879 975 to discuss your timeline.

faqs.question: Do your experts work at Legal Aid rates?
faqs.answer: Yes. All our psychologists and psychiatrists work at Legal Aid rates for publicly funded criminal cases. We understand the budget constraints and provide full fee breakdowns upfront. For privately funded cases, we offer transparent quotes.

faqs.question: Can you provide both a psychologist and psychiatrist for a single case?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Many fitness to plead and risk assessment cases benefit from both perspectives. A psychologist assesses cognitive functioning and psychology. A psychiatrist assesses mental health and medical factors. Your Client Manager will advise on the best combination for your case.

faqs.question: What if the case is very urgent?
faqs.answer: We have a proven track record of delivering even in the most demanding timescales. Call us on 01306 879 975 to discuss your deadline. We offer fast-track services and have experts available at short notice.

faqs.question: How long do assessments take?
faqs.answer: Most fitness to plead assessments take 2â€“4 hours depending on complexity. Risk assessments and pre-sentencing reports may take longer. The expert will advise on the assessment plan during the booking phase.

faqs.question: What happens after the assessment?
faqs.answer: The expert writes a court-ready report addressing all Pritchard criteria or your letter of instruction questions. All reports get 100% quality-checked by our team before delivery. Standard turnaround is 4â€“6 weeks; fast-track is available.

blocks.heading: Ready to Instruct Your Expert?
blocks.bodyText: We'll match you with the right psychologist or psychiatrist in 24 hours. Your Client Manager handles appointments, correspondence, and reports. You focus on your case.
